subject: Physics
reference: JEE Advanced 2026 / IIT Roorkee (Organizing Institute) / jeeadv.ac.in
notes: ~17 questions per subject per paper | CBT mode | Syllabus unchanged from 2025 | Deeper conceptual depth than JEE Main

sub_topics: Units and dimensions — dimensional analysis and applications
Least count and significant figures
Error analysis — absolute, relative, and percentage errors
Vernier callipers and screw gauge — measurement techniques
Determination of g using simple pendulum
Young's modulus — elasticity of the material
Surface tension of water by capillary rise and effect of detergents
Specific heat of a liquid using calorimeter
Focal length of a concave mirror and convex lens using u-v method
Speed of sound using resonance column
Verification of Ohm's law using voltmeter and ammeter
Specific resistance using metre bridge and post office box
Kinematics in one and two dimensions (Cartesian coordinates) — projectile motion
Uniform circular motion and relative velocity
Newton's laws of motion — inertial and uniformly accelerated frames of reference
Static and dynamic friction
Kinetic and potential energy — work and power
Conservation of linear momentum and mechanical energy
Systems of particles — centre of mass and its motion
Impulse — elastic and inelastic collisions
Rigid body — moment of inertia, parallel and perpendicular axes theorems
Moment of inertia of uniform bodies with simple geometrical shapes
Angular momentum, torque, and conservation of angular momentum
Dynamics of rigid bodies with fixed axis of rotation
Rolling without slipping — rings, cylinders, and spheres
Equilibrium of rigid bodies
Collision of point masses with rigid bodies
Forced and damped oscillations in one dimension — resonance
Linear and angular simple harmonic motions
Hooke's law and Young's modulus
Law of gravitation — gravitational potential and field
Acceleration due to gravity — Kepler's laws, geostationary orbits
Motion of planets and satellites in circular orbits — escape velocity
Pressure in a fluid — Pascal's law and buoyancy
Surface energy and surface tension — angle of contact, drops, bubbles, and capillary rise
Viscosity — Stokes' law, terminal velocity, streamline flow
Equation of continuity and Bernoulli's theorem with applications
Modulus of rigidity and bulk modulus
Wave motion — longitudinal and transverse waves, superposition of waves
Progressive and stationary waves — vibration of strings and air columns
Resonance, beats, and speed of sound in gases
Doppler effect in sound
Thermal expansion of solids, liquids, and gases
Calorimetry and latent heat
Heat conduction in one dimension
Elementary concepts of convection and radiation
Newton's law of cooling
Ideal gas laws and absolute scale of temperature
Specific heats — Cv and Cp for monoatomic and diatomic gases
Isothermal and adiabatic processes — bulk modulus of gases
Equivalence of heat and work
First law of thermodynamics and its applications (ideal gases only)
Second law of thermodynamics — reversible and irreversible processes
Carnot engine and its efficiency
Blackbody radiation — absorptive and emissive powers
Kirchhoff's law of thermal radiation
Wien's displacement law and Stefan's law
Coulomb's law — electric field and potential
Electrical potential energy of point charges and dipoles in a uniform electrostatic field
Electric field lines and flux — Gauss's law and applications
Field due to infinitely long straight wire, uniformly charged infinite plane sheet, and thin spherical shell
Capacitance — parallel plate capacitor with and without dielectrics
Capacitors in series and parallel — energy stored in a capacitor
Electric current — Ohm's law
Series and parallel arrangements of resistances and cells
Kirchhoff's laws and simple applications — heating effect of current
Biot-Savart's law and Ampere's law
Magnetic field near a current-carrying straight wire, along axis of a circular coil, and inside a long solenoid
Force on a moving charge and on a current-carrying wire in a uniform magnetic field
Magnetic moment of a current loop — effect of uniform magnetic field on a current loop
Moving coil galvanometer — conversion to voltmeter and ammeter
Electromagnetic induction — Faraday's law and Lenz's law
Self and mutual inductance
RC, LR, LC, and LCR series circuits with DC and AC sources
Electromagnetic waves — nature and characteristics
Electromagnetic spectrum — radio waves, microwaves, infrared, visible, ultraviolet, X-rays, gamma rays
Elementary facts about uses of different regions of the electromagnetic spectrum
Rectilinear propagation of light
Reflection and refraction at plane and spherical surfaces
Total internal reflection
Deviation and dispersion of light by a prism
Thin lenses — combinations of mirrors and thin lenses
Magnification
Wave nature of light — Huygens' principle
Young's double slit experiment — interference and fringe width
Diffraction due to a single slit — diffraction pattern
Polarisation of light — plane polarised light
Brewster's law and Polaroids
Atomic nucleus — composition and size
Alpha, beta, and gamma radiations and their properties
Law of radioactive decay — decay constant, half-life, and mean life
Binding energy and its calculation
Fission and fusion processes — energy released in these processes
Photoelectric effect — Einstein's photoelectric equation
Bohr's theory of hydrogen-like atoms — energy levels and spectral series
Characteristic and continuous X-rays — Moseley's law
de Broglie wavelength of matter waves
Heisenberg's uncertainty principle

subject: Chemistry
reference: JEE Advanced 2026 / IIT Roorkee (Organizing Institute) / jeeadv.ac.in
notes: ~17 questions per subject per paper | Divided into Physical, Inorganic, and Organic Chemistry | Deeper and more extensive than JEE Main

sub_topics: Concept of atoms and molecules — Dalton's atomic theory
Mole concept and chemical formulae
Balanced chemical equations
Stoichiometric calculations — oxidation-reduction, neutralisation, and displacement reactions
Concentration terms — mole fraction, molarity, molality, and normality

major_topics.topic_name: States of Matter: Gases and Liquids
sub_topics: Gas laws and ideal gas equation — absolute scale of temperature
Deviation from ideality — van der Waals equation
Kinetic theory of gases — average, RMS, and most probable velocities and their relation with temperature
Law of partial pressures — diffusion of gases
Intermolecular interactions — types, distance dependence, and effect on properties
Liquids — vapour pressure, surface tension, and viscosity
Bohr's model and spectrum of hydrogen atom
Wave-particle duality — de Broglie hypothesis
Heisenberg's uncertainty principle
Qualitative quantum mechanical picture of hydrogen atom — energies and quantum numbers
Wave function and probability density (plots only) — shapes of s, p, and d orbitals
Aufbau principle, Pauli's exclusion principle, and Hund's rule
Orbital overlap and covalent bond — hybridisation involving s, p, and d orbitals
Molecular orbital energy diagrams for homonuclear diatomic species (up to Ne₂)
Hydrogen bond and polarity in molecules — dipole moment
VSEPR model and shapes of molecules — linear, angular, triangular, square planar, pyramidal, square pyramidal, trigonal bipyramidal, tetrahedral, and octahedral
Intensive and extensive properties — state functions
First law of thermodynamics — internal energy, work (pressure-volume only), and heat
Enthalpy, heat capacity, standard state, and Hess's law
Enthalpy of reaction, fusion, vaporisation, and lattice enthalpy
Second law of thermodynamics — entropy
Gibbs energy — criteria of equilibrium and spontaneity
Law of mass action
Significance of ΔG and ΔG° in chemical equilibrium
Equilibrium constant (Kp and Kc) and reaction quotient
Le Chatelier's principle — effect of concentration, temperature, and pressure
Solubility product and its applications — common ion effect
pH and buffer solutions
Acids and bases — Brønsted and Lewis concepts
Hydrolysis of salts
Electrochemical cells and cell reactions — standard electrode potentials
Electrochemical work and Nernst equation
Electrochemical series — EMF of galvanic cells
Faraday's laws of electrolysis
Electrolytic conductance — specific, equivalent, and molar conductivity
Kohlrausch's law
Primary and secondary batteries — fuel cells
Corrosion — electrochemical basis
Rates of chemical reactions — order and molecularity
Rate law, rate constant, and half-life
Differential and integrated rate expressions for zero and first order reactions
Temperature dependence of rate constant — Arrhenius equation and activation energy
Catalysis — homogeneous and heterogeneous
Activity and selectivity of solid catalysts
Enzyme catalysis and its mechanism
Classification of solids — crystalline state
Seven crystal systems — cell parameters a, b, c, α, β, γ
Close packed structures — cubic (FCC, BCC) and hexagonal (HCP)
Packing in FCC, BCC, and HCP lattices
Nearest neighbours, ionic radii, and radius ratio
Point defects — Schottky and Frenkel defects
Henry's law and Raoult's law — ideal solutions
Colligative properties — lowering of vapour pressure, elevation of boiling point, depression of freezing point, and osmotic pressure
van't Hoff factor and its significance
Adsorption — physisorption and chemisorption
Freundlich adsorption isotherm
Colloids — types, methods of preparation, and general properties
Elementary ideas of emulsions, surfactants, and micelles (definitions and examples only)

major_topics.topic_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
sub_topics: Modern periodic law and the present form of periodic table
Electronic configuration of elements
Periodic trends — atomic radius, ionic radius, ionisation enthalpy, electron gain enthalpy
Periodic trends — valence, oxidation states, electronegativity, and chemical reactivity
Position of hydrogen in the periodic table — occurrence and isotopes
Preparation, properties, and uses of hydrogen
Hydrides — ionic, covalent, and interstitial
Physical and chemical properties of water and heavy water
Hydrogen peroxide — preparation, reactions, structure, and uses
Hydrogen as a fuel
Alkali and alkaline earth metals — reactivity towards air, water, dihydrogen, halogens, and acids
Reducing nature of alkali metals — solutions in liquid ammonia
General characteristics of oxides, hydroxides, halides, and salts of oxoacids
Anomalous behaviour of lithium and beryllium
Compounds of sodium — sodium carbonate, sodium chloride, sodium hydroxide, sodium hydrogen carbonate
Compounds of calcium — calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide, calcium carbonate, calcium sulphate
Oxidation states and trends in chemical reactivity of Groups 13–17
Anomalous properties of B, C, N, O, and F with respect to other group members
Group 13 — reactivity towards acids, alkalis, halogens; borax, orthoboric acid, diborane, BF₃, AlCl₃, alums
Group 14 — reactivity towards water and halogens; allotropes of carbon; CO, CO₂, SiO₂, silicones, silicates, zeolites
Group 15 — allotropes of phosphorus; dinitrogen, ammonia, nitric acid, phosphine, PCl₃, PCl₅; oxides of nitrogen and oxoacids of phosphorus
Group 16 — allotropes of sulfur; dioxygen, ozone, SO₂, H₂SO₄; oxoacids of sulfur
Group 17 (Halogens) — Cl₂, HCl, interhalogen compounds; oxoacids of halogens; bleaching powder
Group 18 (Noble Gases) — chemical properties and uses; compounds of xenon with fluorine and oxygen
Oxidation states and their stability
Standard electrode potentials — interstitial compounds and alloys
Catalytic properties and applications
Preparation, structure, and reactions of oxoanions of chromium (dichromate) and manganese (permanganate)
Lanthanoid and actinoid contractions
Oxidation states of lanthanoids and actinoids
General characteristics and comparison of lanthanoids and actinoids
Werner's theory of coordination compounds
Nomenclature of mononuclear coordination compounds
Cis-trans and ionisation isomerism
Hybridisation and geometries — linear, tetrahedral, square planar, and octahedral
Bonding — Valence Bond Theory (VBT) and Crystal Field Theory (CFT) for octahedral and tetrahedral fields
Magnetic properties (spin-only) and colour of 3d-series coordination compounds
Ligands and spectrochemical series — stability of coordination compounds
Importance, applications, and metal carbonyls
Metal ores and their concentration
Thermodynamic principles of metallurgy — extraction of iron, copper, and zinc
Electrochemical principles of metallurgy — extraction of aluminium
Cyanide process — extraction of silver and gold
Refining of crude metals
Groups I to V — Ag⁺, Hg²⁺, Cu²⁺, Pb²⁺, Fe³⁺, Cr³⁺, Al³⁺, Ca²⁺, Ba²⁺, Zn²⁺, Mn²⁺, and Mg²⁺
Detection of anions — nitrate, halides (excluding fluoride), carbonate, bicarbonate, sulphate, and sulphide
Atmospheric pollution — causes and effects
Water pollution and soil pollution
Industrial waste and its effects
Strategies to control environmental pollution
Green chemistry — principles and applications
Hybridisation of carbon — σ and π bonds
Shapes of simple organic molecules and aromaticity
Structural and geometrical isomerism
Stereoisomers — enantiomers, diastereomers, and meso compounds (up to two asymmetric centres)
Determination of empirical and molecular formulae by combustion method
IUPAC nomenclature of hydrocarbons and their mono- and bi-functional derivatives
Hydrogen bonding effects in organic molecules
Inductive, resonance, and hyperconjugative effects
Acidity and basicity of organic compounds
Reactive intermediates — carbocations, carbanions, and free radicals
Homologous series — physical properties and effect of branching
Conformations of ethane and butane — Newman projections
Preparation from alkyl halides and aliphatic carboxylic acids
Reactions — combustion, halogenation (including allylic and benzylic halogenation), and oxidation
Physical properties — boiling points, density, and dipole moments
Preparation by elimination reactions
Acid-catalysed hydration of alkenes
Metal acetylides
Reactions of alkenes with KMnO₄ and ozone
Reduction of alkenes and alkynes
Electrophilic addition reactions — X₂, HX, HOX (X = halogen)
Effect of peroxide on addition reactions
Cyclic polymerisation reaction of alkynes
Structure of benzene and aromaticity
Electrophilic substitution reactions — halogenation, nitration, sulphonation
Friedel-Crafts alkylation and acylation
Directive influence of substituents in monosubstituted benzene
Physical properties and preparation of phenols
Electrophilic substitution reactions — halogenation, nitration, sulphonation
Reimer-Tiemann reaction and Kolbe reaction
Esterification, etherification, and aspirin synthesis
Oxidation and reduction reactions of phenol
Rearrangement reactions of alkyl carbocations
Grignard reactions
Nucleophilic substitution reactions — SN1 and SN2 and their stereochemical aspects
Physical properties of alcohols
Reactions — esterification, dehydration (formation of alkenes and ethers)
Reactions with sodium, phosphorus halides, ZnCl₂/conc. HCl, and thionyl chloride
Conversion of alcohols into aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids
Preparation of ethers by Williamson's synthesis
C-O bond cleavage reactions of ethers
Preparation of aldehydes and ketones from acid chlorides and nitriles
Aldehydes from esters — benzaldehyde from toluene and benzene
Reactions — oxidation, reduction, oxime and hydrazone formation
Aldol condensation and Cannizzaro reaction
Haloform reaction
Nucleophilic addition with RMgX, NaHSO₃, HCN, alcohol, and amine
Physical properties and preparation from nitriles, Grignard reagents, and hydrolysis of esters and amides
Preparation of benzoic acid from alkylbenzenes
Reactions — reduction, halogenation, formation of esters, acid chlorides, and amides
Preparation of amines from nitro compounds, nitriles, and amides
Hoffmann bromamide degradation and Gabriel phthalimide synthesis
Reaction with nitrous acid — azo coupling reaction of diazonium salts
Sandmeyer and related reactions of diazonium salts
Carbylamine reaction, Hinsberg test, alkylation and acylation
Haloarenes — Fittig and Wurtz-Fittig reactions
Nucleophilic aromatic substitution in haloarenes and substituted haloarenes
Carbohydrates — classification, mono- and disaccharides (glucose and sucrose)
Oxidation, reduction, and glycoside formation of carbohydrates
Hydrolysis of disaccharides — sucrose, maltose, lactose
Anomers
Proteins — amino acids, peptide linkage, structure of peptides (primary and secondary)
Types of proteins — fibrous and globular
Nucleic acids — chemical composition and structure of DNA and RNA
Types of polymerisation — addition and condensation
Homo and copolymers
Natural rubber, cellulose, nylon, Teflon, Bakelite, and PVC
Biodegradable polymers and applications of polymers
Drug-target interaction — therapeutic action of antacids, antihistamines, tranquilisers, analgesics, antimicrobials, and antifertility drugs (excluding structures)
Artificial sweeteners (names only)
Soaps, detergents, and cleansing action
Detection of elements — nitrogen, sulphur, and halogens
Detection and identification of functional groups — hydroxyl (alcoholic and phenolic), carbonyl (aldehyde and ketone), carboxyl, amino, and nitro

subject: Mathematics
reference: JEE Advanced 2026 / IIT Roorkee (Organizing Institute) / jeeadv.ac.in
notes: ~17 questions per subject per paper | Higher abstraction and proof-based problems compared to JEE Main | Syllabus unchanged from 2025

sub_topics: Sets — representations, empty, finite, and infinite sets
Algebra of sets — union, intersection, complement, difference, and symmetric difference
De Morgan's laws on union, intersection, and difference (finite number of sets)
Practical problems based on set operations
Cartesian product of finite sets — ordered pairs
Relations — domain, codomain, and equivalence relation
Functions — domain, codomain, range; invertible, even, odd, into, onto, and one-to-one functions
Special functions — polynomial, trigonometric, exponential, logarithmic, power, absolute value, greatest integer
Sum, difference, product, and composition of functions
Complex numbers — addition, multiplication, conjugation, polar representation
Properties of modulus and principal argument — triangle inequality
Cube roots of unity — geometric interpretations
Fundamental theorem of algebra (statement only)
Quadratic equations with real coefficients — relation between roots and coefficients
Formation of quadratic equations with given roots — symmetric functions of roots
Arithmetic and geometric progressions — arithmetic and geometric means
Sums of finite arithmetic and geometric progressions — infinite geometric series
Sum of first n natural numbers, sums of squares and cubes
Logarithms and their properties
Permutations and combinations
Binomial theorem for positive integral index — properties of binomial coefficients
Matrices as rectangular arrays of real numbers — equality, addition, scalar multiplication, and product
Transpose of a matrix — elementary row and column transformations
Determinant of a square matrix of order up to three — minors and cofactors
Adjoint and inverse of a square matrix of order up to three
Diagonal, symmetric, and skew-symmetric matrices and their properties
Properties of matrix operations
Solutions of simultaneous linear equations in two or three variables
Random experiment and sample space — impossible, simple, and compound events
Addition and multiplication rules of probability
Conditional probability and independence of events
Total probability theorem and Bayes' theorem
Computation of probability using permutations and combinations
Measures of central tendency — mean, median, and mode
Measures of dispersion — mean deviation, standard deviation, and variance of grouped and ungrouped data
Analysis of frequency distributions with same mean but different variance
Random variable — mean and variance of the random variable
Trigonometric functions — periodicity and graphs
Addition and subtraction formulae
Formulae involving multiple and sub-multiple angles
General solution of trigonometric equations
Inverse trigonometric functions — domain, range, and principal values
Properties of inverse trigonometric functions
Relations between sides and angles of a triangle
Sine rule, cosine rule, half-angle formulae, and area of a triangle
Cartesian coordinates — distance between two points, section formulae, shift of origin
Equation of a straight line in various forms — angle between two lines, distance of a point from a line
Lines through intersection of two given lines
Equation of a circle — standard, general, and parametric forms
Intersection of a circle with a straight line or another circle
Equation of a circle through intersection of two circles
Equations of a parabola, ellipse, and hyperbola in standard forms
Parametric equations, focus, directrix, latus rectum of parabola, ellipse, and hyperbola
Tangent and normal to parabola, ellipse, and hyperbola
Locus problems
Real-valued functions of a real variable — domain and range
Limit, continuity, and differentiability of functions
Limit and continuity of the sum, difference, product, and quotient of functions
L'Hôpital's rule and evaluation of limits
Derivatives of standard functions and their chain rule applications
Derivatives of polynomial, rational, trigonometric, inverse trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions
Derivatives of implicit functions — parametric differentiation
Second order derivatives
Rolle's theorem and Lagrange's mean value theorem
Applications of derivatives — tangents and normals, increasing and decreasing functions
Maxima and minima using first and second derivative tests
Integration as the inverse process of differentiation
Standard integrals involving algebraic, trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions
Integration by substitution, by parts, and by partial fractions
Integration using trigonometric identities
Definite integrals and their properties
Fundamental theorem of integral calculus
Application of definite integrals — area between curves
Formation of ordinary differential equations
Solution of homogeneous differential equations
Linear first order differential equations — integrating factor method
Variable separable method
Addition of vectors — triangle and parallelogram laws
Scalar multiplication of a vector
Scalar triple product and its interpretation as volume
Dot product and cross product of two vectors — geometrical interpretations
Properties of dot and cross products
Applications — work done by a force and torque
Direction cosines and direction ratios of a line
Equation of a straight line in space — Cartesian and vector forms
Skew lines — distance between two skew lines
Equation of a plane in different forms — Cartesian and vector
Angle between a line and a plane — angle between two planes
Distance of a point from a plane
Intersection of a line and a plane
